text: I have been getting threats nd private calls and i spoke to a customer service representative 2 nights ago to change my number the CSR said they can charge the fee of $15 to my bill payment and that my number will be changed the next day in the morning but my voice mails and txt messages will be deleted.. its weird because it has never happened before and Its been 3 days now that my number has not been changed !! I called once again a couple minutes ago and now they tell me that they will not change my number untill i pay the $15 i know $15 is not much but im a young person that has many bills and im just fed up of the threats and private calls . The customer service representative i talked to 30 min ago was rude and did not even care ! Other people i have talked to before about questions ive had were very nice but this 2 customer service representatives have been rude nd lied to a customer. And i should know because i am a CSR customer service representative as well for 3 years now !!! I am very disappointed!!!

Had a worst experience with T Mobile. Made us switch to t-mobile from at&t by promising way too many things but when it's time to deliver, they backed off on everything. I called the customer support and escalated to the supervisor and she said 'I'm sorry you were given mis information, but I cant do anything to help you`. The information was given by her own staff.

I have been a long time Tmobile client living in a big city on east coast. 
Their telephone coverage is not satisfactory, too many drop calls. They keep promising; update is to be completed for months.. We  have been waiting patiently, still not a slightest improvement. 
Today I have talked to a customer rep and a supervisor, Dissapointing experience to say the least. First one said she made the name correction on the account second one said that was not done, rI must go to a store in person to correct a typo that was done by them.... Second one first said she will send me a rate letter explaining the rate change, wrote my email address and by the end of 15 minutes that  turns out not to be accurate, as there was not price increase in fact on per minute international phone charges.
What is so unbelievable is that INTERNATIONAL CHARGES. If you are in Europe making international call will cost you only $0.20 roaming charges, nothing else, very good price. HOWEVER, If you make the same phone call from USA that same minute will cost you $3...  Explanation is they have good deals with carriers there in Europe and prices in USA is different. Total nonsense.

We switched to a different service recently because T-mobile kept dropping calls, especially in the new area I'm moving to. I wanted to unlock my old 4-year old phone from T-mobile, and customer service was horrible. I cycled through 3 representatives. One hung up on me, and every single one told me something different. I had to trouble my dad (the account-holder) to stay on the line with me because they said they needed him ... Then, they told us after 45 minutes or so that they couldn't unlock my phone unless my dad went to a t-mobile shop and showed his ID because we didn't remember or hadn't set up some obscure PIN number. We offered to e-mail our IDs or tell them other personal information, but no. Apparently, unlocking a god damn old phone requires the upmost security measures. Are you serious?

T Mobile is the worst cell phone service I had. I never recommend anyone for T Mobile.  Their program for device are scam.  Never trust this company.
There are many other cheap and good service providers available.

I thought T Mobile is cheap but not.  Everything is scam. 
The customer service manager Mr.David is very rude.

I have been with them over 18 months, they were disaster and lie too much with the service, from the store to financial department all are worst. I am planning to move to some other service as they are scam. Charged extra 11$ per month since last 18 months for upgrade, now they are saying its only for Insurance. 
The lie about building up credit, before purchase they confirmed my credit will increase if I pay on time for the device which I purchase for %29 per month for 24 month. I paid off after 18 months and finally understand its not for building up credit.

I mail money order payments. They could cash and apply the payment for february but not January. I have been given different information by every rep I've talked to. They should look for a missing payment, period. They want me to jump through hoops with money gram and them. I find it shady.

T Mobil is the worst phone service out. It’s not to bad over seas but here in the states it’s the worse. They will try to cheat you out of your money if you don’t pay close attention to the bill. I had paid off my iPhone 6 and they were still trying to charge me an extra $20 each bill I had to  call twice about. Than I went over seas and had the T Mobil international plan and called them to make sure I was using the phone correctly over the WiFi and they told me some bs and ended charging me $700 for that bill. Than switched from sprint to T Mobil. T mobile was supposed to pay over the sprint bill of $ 508, I sent them the paper work to pay it twice and they still didn’t two years later. T Mobil needs to go out of business. Worse cell phone company out. They are trash. They get a 0 for effort from me.

I swirched  from at&t to tmobile to save money. And I am annoyed . My calls have never been dropped so much n my life.  It takes forever to connect a call and when I do it's choppy. I regret this descions and looking 2 move again. Don't be fooled by any promotion it's not worth the headache.

The worst company I have had to deal they make all means to reap u of your money when I finish leasing my phone they said I was owing them more money and then gave me a tablet and said was for free but I have been paying for a tablet that is not working

As a loyal tmobile customer of 12 years, I'm sad to say that this company has ultimately gone right down the drain. These days their service is mediocre at best, but they claim to be cheaper than most other companies, which is supposed to be a perk, right? However, their JUMP program is a complete scam and when their sales representatives give false information to make a sale the company refuses to make things right. Their phone promotions are only meant to reel in new unsuspecting victims and do not apply to existing customers. Long term customers are treated like garbage because I believe Tmobile figures they've already got you and that you're not going anywhere, much like an abusive relationship. Their retention team is an absolute joke too. When I told them we were considering moving to another company because of the way we had been treated their response was that we better hurry and leave before a certain date so we didn't get charged for another month. Hands down the worst customer service ever. They even overcharged us on our final bill, but we're on to bigger and better things now. Thanks AT&T!
Store #2070 manger John Wilson is rude and his co worker Sally broke my son screen today this behavior was unacceptable don't go in this store!!!
I went into the store in Florence, Alabama and Lindsey Davis was the best! She explained what I was getting charged for in great details!

T-mobile is the worst ! How many employees who do not know what they do, who work only out of necessity and not because they love what they do.
I did not have service during the whole month of December, I contacted them to cancel my account, I asked the employee how much is my balance. He tells me that there is a balance of $ 158.00. I paid them on January 23 and 20 days later, on February 13, 2018 to be specific. I received a convergent letter from a collection company telling me that t-mobile had sent me to the collection because according to them I owe $ 234 dollars. That was generated because I had a balance of $ 70.00 owed and because I canceled my account on February 8, 2018 and the cycle closed on February 5, 2018, they charged me $ 164.00 for 3 days of service. When I called to try to solve the problem, they told me that the system was damaged and that they would call me and never did, in my effort to resolve the situation I went back and contacted them. Jacquelyn was the person who worked hard to help me, trying to get her supervisor to authorize a discount of 34 dollars, so I only had to pay 200.00 and he refused. That supervisor was very smart to despise the only $ 200 I was willing to pay and it was the only thing I had at the moment. He preferred to send me to the collection.
 I want to let the supervisor know that if that employee had been doing his job, we would have avoided all this. When I called, he would have given me the correct balance and he would have informed me that, if I canceled, I should do it one day before the cycle closed and I would avoid being charged $ 164.00 for three days of service.I am a representative of customer service and I do my job with passion and I assure that my clients are satisfied with the service. T-mobile open your eyes If your employees don’t believe in your business, the quality of their work will be poor.

Compared to Sprint that i had they have been wonderful, polite and helpful....also saving me a lot of money.... I admit I have only just started with them, but so far everything they have promised they have done...Thank you T-Mobile

Cannot stand this company and their hidden deceptive tactics to lie to customers. I mistakenly went back to them after being a loyal customer and getting burned once, trusting Again that they wouldn't deceive me and I'm stuck paying huge bills for nothing. I'm a single mom who cannot afford these charges and am sick of their terrible service. It makes me sick to make payments to them and I wish I could afford for them to rot in collections.

Had one of the most hair raising experiences in a TMO store.  I was actually traumatized after I left.  Cut in line by managers friends after waiting an hour.  It was worse than the RI DMV!  

Call them and you get to play the shell game of how much will I pay for my line which never seems to actually pan out.  Totally disastrous customer service focussed on share price and profits.

This company has the WORST customer service ever. I have called five times, been transferred over 13 times, hung up on 3 times all in an effort to get an account I never activated closed!! I have spent over 3 hours on the phone with these lying reps. I would NEVER EVER consider them anything but lying crooks. I do not understand why the FCC doesn't do something like shut them down. Horrible experience and still not resolved even though they have all their equipment back and I never even opened the Sim cards or phones. Totally sealed.

9Horrible and disgusting! Signed up for veterans discount IN STORE and had to fight every month to get it. .it's amazing how the customer service  (joke ) speak to people and after 5 months they don't have a veterans discount. ..I guess the info on their website is completely fraudulent. ..going to verizon as this is not an issue. ...I don't know how they're in business. ..never again with this nasty company..thieving  scumbags

Recently I signed up for T-Mobile for single life. I have the 55 plus plan. In addition I pay $25 or T-Mobile one plan allowing me to have unlimited hotspot at 4 G speed. 
My issue is that I was sold on a ZTE quartz watch phone. The watch was $96. The problem is the store did not have it in stock. Online does not have it in stock either. I was told that since they don't have the ZTE I could go for one of their other watches all of which are $400. This is an increase of $300 over the ZTE watch.
When I explained that selling someone on something that they don't have and trying to have them take something much more expensive is a classic bait-and-switch they suggested I look in other stores for the watch. 
After checking with seven different stores in my area it turns out no one has this phone and they have not had it for some time. Get all stores keep it in their demonstration window and continue to sell the idea of owning that watch.
I have tried to do every solution I could to work out a equitable solution for each party that T-Mobile has not given into anything. I even offered to purchase the display model I was denied. Asked if they would discount the other models I was denied. I need to get this watch as I have two devices to complete my agreement with T-Mobile to pay off my former two devices from Sprint. However T-Mobile is not helping at all. Be very careful with T-Mobile and what they promised.

Customers living in the US should only speak to customer service representatives that are located in the US and not outside the US. The call centers in the Philippines should be shut down immediately and permanent , the Filipino representatives are rude impatient disrespectful untrained untrustworthy has poor English doesn't understand plain simple English or the customers needs. They get frustrated and hang up on you I have been hung up on several times , by Filipino representatives especially when I ask to speak with their manager or supervisor. We shouldn't have to struggle to understand the representatives that doesn't speak clear proper English. It's a language barrier foreign countries should take customer service calls only for foreign countries not for the US. TMobile should make it easier to get the phone you really want without any deposits down payments credit checks and bill us for the phone monthly that we selected. Credit checks down payments and deposits should be eliminated immediately the customers credit shouldn't determine if a deposit and down payment is needed in order to get a name brand phone. All phones that are not name brand phones do not require any deposits down payments or credit checks and that's not fair because , as a loyal customer you shouldn't feel like your phone provider only cares about how much money they can make off of you. We're told that our service is completely unlimited with unlimited data but we're slowed down after we reached our monthly gigabyte due to the cluttering caused by lack of towers and the amount of money we spend monthly shows that T-Mobile don't care if you go broke as long as they are getting paid. T-Mobile only have around 3000 towers compared to AT&T 10,000 plus towers and the $15 a month for supposedly having unlimited international calling is bogus especially when the country you want to call isn't listed on the extremely short list , there's a international calling service called CallnRoam that provide 5000 minutes for $14.99 a month so why would I pay T-Mobile the same price and be charged more money per minute depending on what country I call and if it's a landline phone or mobile phone. This is the slogan from CallnRoam "The best calling rates to the Philippines are with us. Our Telebabad Kabayan plan offers you 5,000 minutes to call the Philippines at a flat, $14.99 rate for 30 days. Stop thinking about how much it'll cost you to stay in touch: with CallnRoam you get what you deserve". You would think since T-Mobile is a foreign company they would have more coverage and customers in foreign countries than the United States but it seems like the American Dollar always wins , the customer service calls are being routed to the Philippines and Qatar but there's no T-Mobile service or towers in these countries so how can you hire call centers in foreign countries that doesn't use your service because you want provide it to them ? Foreign Representatives living in foreign countries cannot fully provide assistance or understand how we feel even though they claim to understand how we feel but they don't because , they're not a T-Mobile customer and they want to be a T-Mobile customer but T-Mobile is too selfish to provide their service in the foreign countries their call centers are located in.

Had a jump on my phone. When it came to tge time to use it, nithibg but tmobile mistakes. 7 days later still without a phone. First time they sent a damaged phone to replace my damaged  phone. Second time, they processed the claim wrong and the store cannot release the phone. In this day and age, they have to resend the phone!!!! Because of the wrong filling of the claim. It is truly unbelievable!!! I have to wait for 3 more days now.

My name is Deshanaye Alexander and I created this account just so I can let T-mobile know how FANTASTIC of a customer service rep they have. I have went through many reps the past couple of days. I had finished paying off my leases and wanted to get a new phone and also wanted to add a line to my current plan. Not one person I spoke to before Kristina could give me the right answer. I spoke to people both in the Tmobile store and over the phone and I was getting told different things. I called this morning and I got Kristina as my representative. I explained my situation, I explained what I thought didn't make sense and she understood. She took the time out of her morning to go through several motions to make sure I have got the right deal. She found out what the problem was and she MADE SURE that she FIXED IT! She got the job done promptly. She was very respectful and wanted to make sure she could do everything she needed to help me out. After the results....BOY DID SHE REALLY HELP ME OUT! Kristina was the ONLY one that was able to figure out what the issue was. She did not try to argue with me, she did not tell me that I was wrong...she simply listened to what my issue was and fixed it. If it wasn't for Kristina I probably would not be a customer with Tmobile anymore. I would love to speak to more representatives like her! SHE REALLY NEEDS A PROMOTION OR NEEDS TO BE A MANAGER ASAP! She is great in so many ways that I can't explain and I can't thank her enough for what she has done for me and my family. THANK YOU SOOOOO MUCH Kristina. If anyone has an issue, BELIEVE ME she would get it done. I have never wrote a review about a representative and she did so much for me I couldn't go without acknowledging her greatness. ASK FOR KRISTINA she gets the job DONE.

The plans are great, but that is all.  When I ordered the service and my phone, I had the representative check the service in my area, and I checked the coverage map, everything looked good.  In reality, my phone was useless.  Customer service was worse than the coverage.  The technical support team said that there was nothing to do because they didn't have good coverage in this area.  Every time you call them you have to put the phone number and last 4 of your SSN in the system, and then you have to immediately give the same info to the agent.  About half the time you have to repeat the information when they switch you to another department.  To get a return label, it took me 3 tries before they could get it to me and I had to give them my email each time.  Interesting thing is that when I placed the order, I received at least 6 or 7 emails with the status.  If you don't have a local store or know someone that uses their service in the areas you will need it, I would RUN the other direction.

I wish I could give no stars. We had our identity stolen and iPhone financed in our name. Tmobile also allowed our number to be ported out to MetroPCS where the thief then got into our bank and stole money. Google it bc we aren't the first to have our tmobile info hacked. That was bad but dealing with TMobile has made a bad situation worse. For four days my husband and I have spent hours on the phone with this company. Each time  we get someone new, we start at square one. We were promised call backs that never happened. We were promised that the illegally purchased cells would be shut down and removed from our bill which has not happened. Its been four days and our phone number is still not ours. Today I called and was told no fraud had been reported! It's a nightmare. I cant believe a company can care so little about its customer of over four years. Our bank was extremely helpful. Please research Tmobile hacks. And while it can happen at another company, just know that Tmobile has done nothing in the four days we have reported this to help us or cancel things or get our number back. Obviously security is an issue and each tmobile rep is hoping to just push you off and you give up calling and calling. But I wont!

Tmoblie processed an authorized payment on 12/15/17 it cleared then on 12/21/17 they processed the same payment that was unauthorized. This caused the authorized payment on 12/22 and 3 other credit cards payments to be returned 2 times as NSF. I only put money in this account to pay bills. Now 31 days later I am still not able to get them to pay me back. My phone is not working and I can not get a replacement until I PAY a bill that would be taken care of it they would pay/apply my bank fees to my tmoblie account. My bank has supplied them with documentation on all the fees when applied and the continued rate that will be charged. This still is not good enough!!! The oversea customer service reps & supervisors are completely worthless as they don't undetstand our banking laws and customer rights.

Horrible network, Cant even load google search page.
Hey I have T-mobile network, use your maps mine doesn't load data, it sucks .

With T-Mobile we got poor to no reception so we changed carriers, I called T-Mobile several times to get a final bill to take into our new carrier to get reimbursed, T-Mobile gave me the run arround every time! We finally got the final bill and I call to pay it and they’ve turned it over to collections in less than a month! T-Mobile has the worst customer service I ever known, save yourself the frustration and when you see a T-Mobile store run the opposite direction!!
Very bad service by the company

My daughter was in Japan unable to make a call or text, so I called customer service  at 10:30 pm. I was surprised they were able to contacted her in Japan they fixed her communication problem. I am very pleased with t mobile técnical support!

Don't fall for their gimmick. They tell you one thing then you are screwed once you sign up.  I was told they would pay off my existing contract with my carrier and now 6 months later and fighting them on it they still have not paid.  They say they have no record of telling me that yet it's advertised everywhere.  The tax free well that's not true either. There are taxes added on my bill.  Then they block you from your account so you have to call in and speak to someone and they charge you to speak with someone.  Then you pay your bill they shut your service off anyway.  They find ways to get you for your money. Dont trust them and dont do business with them.  It's all a scam.

My experience with T-Mobile was - and continues to be - a nightmare. Useless (to us) service, endlessly dropped calls, impotent customer service, unanswered letters, confused billing, and on and on. It just never stops and they seem uninterested. 
When choosing a cellular service, remember - and share - my story.
NEVER T-MOBILE.
Called customer care, and it only took about 30 seconds from when I dialed the number for the rep to pick up. The nicest rep and the best customer service I've ever had!

I have been customer of Family plan for 6 lines with T-Mobile since 2005. Never missed a bill since then.  It’s about 11 years now. This company is growing like weed on the grass. The services are so much but all below average. The website is so lossy. The website usually has a a slow response such that you get tired and give up and leave the site!
The connection is poor in most places. The customer service 611 usually does not resolve your problem or does it partially.
 In general, if I were in 11 years ago position and understanding, never, ever pick TMobile!!!!
No I have ballences on my bills due to phone purchase by my kids that I will pay them off and leave TMobile for good. 
Never, ever pick TMobile!!! It is a very dump carrier !!!! 
Professor Rooz
Texas
Jan 2 ,2018

I have been a T-Mobile customer for years and always paid bill on time or early.  Dropped calls increased greatly to the point that I was hesitant to take a phone call and had to forewarn and apologize to family and friends that it could happen during our call.  It even happened when I was on the phone with their customer service people.  Enough is enough!  So I just switched service providers.  Wow!  What a difference!

Also, T-Mobile would not refund money due me of unused service time.  I'm not one to write reviews, but this is an exception.  Suggest you not use T-Mobile.

I have been a loyal T-mobile customer for over a decade and was trying to upgrade my phone to the newest model. I was given a massive run around, being told by a supervisor that I would be receiving a phone free of charge, yet when it came time to talk to someone about the phone being sent, I was told that "the promise wasn't on record" despite having been told it was less than five minutes ago. When I attempted to explain the situation to the customer service representative, she continued to talk over me, ignoring what I was saying. Finally, I hung up.

If I could have give no tars I would have every time I try to put money on my phone tryed everything keeps telling me site is down when I phone get so far then cut of mobile or house phone pathetic been like this several days

The only reason I am giving five stars is because of Byron from Albany. He saved me a lot of money when 6 other people I spoke to were not helpful at all. The only reason I stayed with the company is because of Byron from Albany or else I would have switched. Shoutout to Byron!!!

There are not enough characters available to convey just how bad t-mobile is.  I have been a loyal customer for 10 years and just last week decided it was time to consolidated mine and my childrens' plans under one carrier.  So I requested an unlock to my phone so I could transfer my service to ATT.  That was on a Wednesday and it is now Sunday.  Still can't use the ATT Sim card because the phone is still locked.  And what's worse is that every time I call I get a different answer and description of how the unlock should work.  It's been the worst service experience I've ever had.

Do not go to T-Mobile the customer service will tell you anything then you get stuck with a high bill and they do not care, not even the managers. The service is not what it use to be and I've been a customer since 1999. I'm definitely looking else where and I'm sure T-Mobile does not care!

i would give 5 stars if danielle was the only one to help me - and 2 others whom i recommended to TMobile - move over to tmobile. i went to the 30 ave astoria tmobile and got speedy service. so i naturally recommended my brother who took out 2 lines also. however we discovered that Adam messed up IMEIs and colors so returning 2 lines had major issues. were it not for danielle we'd be leaving more reviews. bad reviews. it is not recommended to go to this tmobile unless you have danielle as the take charge person. the one thing you need is honesty, not luring customers in and misleading them. we felt adam did this. with free Netflix plus the mega mix up which tmobile took many days to sort out and only at Danielle's vouching for us.

Very bad experience!!! I was talked into switching to their company from verizon by 1 of their representatives and that was a very bad mistake!!! I have called and talked to someone about my problem and every single person I have talked to told me something different...Whoever is thinking about having service through this company better be careful and make sure you get details because they failed to mention them to me and now I'm stuck with them until I pay my phones off. As soon as I do I'm done with them!!! I learned my lesson!!!

After my phone was activated and I realized I had no service I called their customer service line. I was told to go to the next town over to make calls because the service was better in that area. I'll never give this this company another penny.

Terrible customer service.  Changing carriers.  They make a mistake in keying something in and can't fix it without a trip to the specific store that make the error.  Would give them 0 stars if I could for rudeness of the store personnel who make the mistake to begin with.

Someone from t-mobile needs to try to contact your value customer because I did not know that they took advantage of  senior citizens when it comes to refunding moneiesback to their customers or give other  Options.

From the day we switched to T-Mobile from our original carrier, it's been a nightmare.  Wrong information , discounts not applied and extremely low trade in values applied to our original phones. For example, Verizon would give us $212 for our trade in where T mobile would offer only $119. When I called T Mobile for the 5th time, the supervisor basically said sorry we made a mistake but too bad.  You don't get the discount you deserve. T-mobile consistently passes me on to another dept who will contradict the information that I was given. I'm currently on another hour long battle with them.

These people think that we have time to be calling and holding for morw than 30 min. I had claimed the insurance for a phone they ended up bloking the phone i was using and send me the same phone that was currently working i send it back asked for a refund and to this day have not recived refund i ended up switching to another company. Wose customer serves no one can help you they think money isnt a problem.before i changed i paid the account in full and now apparently i have a past due amount. I cant even deal with them anymore ive called like 5 times in 1 month and never get my problem resolved.

Hi,
First when you are new to T-Mobile, they are very polite, until you become their customer. Once you become their customer that’s where they start looting.
I was charged extra money saying that I have some international calls made from my mobile. When called customer care, they don’t even have patience to listen and answer the questions. Now I regret choosing T- Mobile. Out of experience please think twice before you want to switch to t-mobile.

"Now days it is difficult to find a good cellphone company that won't lie, or cheat you in some way, we all know this to be true by now. Verizon, T-Mobile, and metro PCs have me it clear to, not just me, that they couldn't care less about their customers. I have been with each of these "carriers" for minimum 3 years each! Believe me or not, I've been cheated and lied to, or they tired to steal and extra some of money from my account (long story) but my real point here is we need one.. yes folks, one cellphone company. Not two, or three, but one. With three different companies the possibilities are spread vary thin. My most recent at T-Mobile was, I came in due to the fact my screen stopped working, but couldn't access my account because I wasn't in authority for my own account. Casey explained how it would cost the company more to repair my phone instead of buying a new one. Also Casey told me he would sell me two phone for the price of one, I gladly accepted this offer, and when to open a new account, but when Casey returned from the back room he only came out with one phone, I proceeded with asking him where my second phone was, and he explained "oh that phone you already have is your second phone" after that I thanked Casey for is HONEST, GREAT service and walked out of the store to never look back at this dishonest company every again Casey you ****, and your a **** for that. To who ever owns T-Mobile you don't know how to run a Honest business or a business. Hope this helps because intended to spread and share more of my stories

Complete garbage phones. Got the TMobile revvl and it never picks up wifi or Bluetooth and won't let me recieve calls from other providers which is done on purpose to get your friends over to crap mobile

Do not believe their promotions. Last year, there was a "customer appreciation" - 2 free lines (for the life of the lines) and 2 free tablets. --Do not take advantage of any promotion if you already have a promotion on your lines. It will change your rate plan and undo any promotion you may have existing. They will bill you backdated items to the time of the previous promo and demand payment. Long story short. They didn't even make good on the 2 lines and 2 tablets being free. They are charging me equipment for tablets.

I don't like them, they said we had coverage in our area in which we do not. We switched and never got a bill and they would not take the brand new phones back. And they sent us to collections. To my Husband and I they are the worst and lie about coverage just to get money from you.....
I did this already
I did this already

Hey everyone,  just a heads up that T-Mobile will lie to you and refuse to unlock a phone that you own for any reason they can come up with.
I was lied to repeatedly by different customer service representatives about being able to unlock my phone I had purchased through T-Mobile.  I was told at first that I would be able to unlock the phone even though I was still paying because I was military, then I was told I had to wait 2 weeks, then I was told I needed to pay off the $721 I owed on the phone . After I paid off the phone I was told they could not unlock it because I had to have it active on the plan in the US for 40 days. Me and my husband are stationed over seas so this is impossible,  I had made all this very clear to them from the begining and they continued to lie and say that it wouldn't be a problem and tricked me into buying a phone I cannot use.  So just be aware T-Mobile exploits and lies to military members.

Hey everyone,  just a heads up that T-Mobile will lie to you and refuse to unlock a phone that you own for any reason they can come up with.
I was lied to repeatedly by different customer service representatives about being able to unlock my phone I had purchased through T-Mobile.  I was told at first that I would be able to unlock the phone even though I was still paying because I was military, then I was told I had to wait 2 weeks, then I was told I needed to pay off the $721 I owed on the phone . After I paid off the phone I was told they could not unlock it because I had to have it active on the plan in the US for 40 days. Me and my husband are stationed over seas so this is impossible,  I had made all this very clear to them from the begining and they continued to lie and say that it wouldn't be a problem and tricked me into buying a phone I cannot use.  So just be aware T-Mobile exploits and lies to military members.

T-Mobile has paid 8 billion for a new network operating on a 600mhz frequency. Nonetheless they are selling phones, including the iPhone, that cannot access this frequency. Therefore, you are paying up to #1,000 for. phone that is already obsolete in T-Mobile. Of course they do not tell you this. 

Besides their coverage is horrible.
Great service!

My buddy and i shared a plan and he decided to switch to Tmobile and they promised to pay off his phone on my account. Well here we 3 months later and they have yet to take care of his phone. I've had TMobile for 14 years and have seen them improve greatly in the last 6 of those. I've always lived in an urban/suburban area and have had solid signal with very good coverage and data speeds. The LTE speeds in the 3-4 years have been excellent. Their customer service approach (teams with regional focus and follow-up to resolution) is far better than the past, and I had no reason to believe that would change according to inner reports from friends who are employees.
In September of 2017 I noticed a drastic change in customer service policy/procedure - more calls to 611 than in the past were routed to overseas call centers who 1) had little ability/authority to resolve matters 2) struggled with the English language in their communication 3) couldn't relate to social and practical issues like addresses that directly affect US customers - all of which became more of a 'reading off a script' experience and endless cyclical talking that went nowhere.   In addition, any Twitter support through DirectMessaging was focused on hyper positivity, lofty rhetoric, exaggerated claims of expertise, and writing too much about themselves in an attempt to sympathize and empathize in obvious relatability - all which would be fine if accompanied with competence, accuracy, attention to detail, follow through, and a timely resolution. Because of these changes in customer service approach/policy, issues that formerly took days to a week to resolve now become lingering problems that last weeks to a month or more.

I moved to a suburban/rural area that is still within a couple miles of a larger city, and according to antenna search/locator sites and an app called Network Cell Info Lite, there are several T-Mobile towers in my area, from 1/2 to 4 miles away with only one obstructed by geography (foothill). I never connect to the closest 4G tower, leading me to believe it's inactive or non-functioning. Doing drive tests with the diagnostic app reveal a very weak signal being br oadcast by most of the towers in the area, and even with full signal reception on phone (bars) the latency is very bad and data speeds are horrible and subpar - more akin to that of 3G or less.  
All of this information has been passed and communicated to TMobile over the past 4 weeks with little more than 'there are geographical difficulties' being reported from the engineers in the field.  This is only true of 1 out of the 6+ towers in the area.  I understand business decisions of cost vs benefit, and that unless/until a large customer base was complaining of problems they would delay or refrain from addressing such issues, but it seems there exists a lack of maintenance and ongoing upgrade concern/competence.  All the happy-talk virtue signaling in the world from customer service reps doesn't alter that.

So I'm now experiencing what many others in 'less than adequate' coverage areas have experienced and the apparent changes in customer support have only exacerbated the frustration and unacceptable conditions.  This is my 2Cents.
You can't trust their coverage map claims which are misleading, false, and blatantly full of errors.  
However, if you live in an area where towers are maintained and the signal is strong, their service is one of the best.  They really need to think through and work on their customer service approach - once again.

**UPDATE 11/07/2017**
After multiple attempts to contact a customer care or technical support person within the US by phone, I got lucky and reached a first level tech support person who transferred me to the 2nd level support. They took extensive notes and then created a ticket to be sent to engineers - meanwhile I created such an uproar on public Twitter messages that I was contacted by DirectMessage and they requested to call me directly to resolve the problem. 
During all of this, I continued to search for answers/solutions online. None of the support personnel directed or linked me to any T-Mobile online support documentation - but in my searches I found what I thought might be the problem. There was a detailed T-Mobile support document that did indeed provide some helpful information and could've been forwarded or linked or sent to me within the first day or two. 
Solution? The Personal Cellspot 4G LTE mini tower needed a complete 'reset', which can be accomplished by holding down the reset button on the back of the mini tower for 20+ seconds.  Up to this point, I had only tried 10 and 15 seconds as well as unplugging it for 30 minutes.  Once I did the 20+ second reset, the data speeds returned to a normal level (80-90mbits down, 30-45mbits up) and all was well.  I relayed all of this information - including the support documentation URL to T-Mobile support teams on Twitter and phone support and hopefully they will begin a process of searching for 'known issues' and FAQ's while assisting customers in order to actually resolve issues and to learn themselves in the process.

I was really upset with T-Mobile in 2017.  Previous review is below.  However, I waited for the next promotion, and took advantage of the BOGO offer.  Their service has been great, no connection issues, so I have change my rating.  The important thing to remember regarding my previous issue (below), is to alway get the name of the person you talked to.  Confirm things you are told over the phone with a supervisor, and the the supervisor's name.  This way, if you have a problem later, you can say who you spoke to and it will give your case credibility.

I was so angy, I initially gave 1 star for T-Mobile due to below issue:
Over the phone, I called T-Mobile to switch from AT&T to T-Mobile based on their good prices and the fact they were doing a BOGO promotion on the Note 8.  I was assured I could take advantage of the Note 8 promotion when I received the sim cards.   I specifically asked about this because I was concerned about the promotion ending before I get the sim cards.  The girl assured me that it would still be available to me when I get the sim cards.   So, a week later I get the sim cards and go into a T-Mobile corporate store to install the sim cards and port my numbers over.  I told them I wanted to take advantage of the Note 8 promotion as well.  They say the promotion ended 2 days ago, so nothing they can do.  I called T-Mobile and tried to fight this injustice, and no matter how high up I went in rank, the end result was there was nothing they can do for me.   So, basically, they got me to sign up over the phone (30 minutes) and run my social security number, spent 1 hour at the T-Mobile Store, and 1 Hour on the phone after I left the store.  All a waist of time.  I cancelled my service with them, and now I have to drive 20 minutes back to their store to drop off the sim cards to get refunded for those.  You would think they would do something.  It's just very wrong and unfair, and a waste of time.

All of them put me to hold again, after which nobody picked up.

Horrible customer service. Operators are in the Philippines. Language barrier and limited power. T-Mobile charged my account and I don’t have service with them. If they can’t help you - they pass you around or hang up on you. Stay Verizon for life.

I have to say T-Mobile customer service is really BAD BAD BAD.I worked in customer service for 41 years and I know employees are not getting proper training or the employees are just mentally challenged. Also they cannot speak English you can understand. ITS TIME TO RUN OFF THE PERSON RESPONSIBLE FOR THIS PROBLEM.

I only made 1 phone call before canceling my account now I get a bill for $320

I tried to get a replacement phone but I'm on the no credit plan. However, I have made 12 consecutive payments and so I am eligible for a new phone with zero down. I called and was transferred 8 times, and basically every other person told me I could totally get a new phone with zero down and just a monthly extra charge for the phone, and the the other representatives told me it was not possible unless I upgraded my whole plan to a plan that would charge me $40 more! All I wanted was to get a new phone and tack on the extra charge to my bill, but they would not do it. And to top it all on my 5th time being transferred the representative "April" was extremely rude to me and started yelling over the phone telling me I had no other option but to get the new more expensive plan! I am seriously considering changing providers over this. 8 transfers 1.5 hours and nothing done!

DO NOT TRUST T-MOBILE, NEVER NEVER BELIEVE T-MOBILE REPS, THEY ARE ALL LIARS. I recently transferred from at&t, the customer service said i can use my old iphone but i need to LEASE a phone prior to unlocking my at&t phone. After my phone was unlocked i tried returning the LEASE phone and they told me it cannot be returned and i have to pay the total cost of the LEASE PHONE. They transferred me from one dept to another, around 3-4 diff depts, they are telling m different things. ALL OF THEM LIED they tell you they’ll pay for your phone but without your consent they’ll force the new phone on you with their addl charges.

Spent 4 hours in a store switching our family plan to a business plan and they failed miserably at it. The slimy salesman kept telling me they are saving me money every time I asked why it was taking so long. They had me pay off a phone and upgrade another phone and when I called T-Mobile the next day, they added lines and other equipment that I didn't have or own on my bill adding an additional $80 a month to my bill. Now I have spent a year dealing with the bs and still no resolution, just a constant run around, besides getting hung up on all the time. I spend at least an hour on the phone every month trying to solve the screwed mess they have made. Then I log into the website and no documents or past bills are available for my viewing, hmmmm, wonder why? That's how messed up this company is!

If i could give less than 1 star I would. I was a Verizon customer for 25+ years. Never any major issues, great coverage, helpful customer service, problems resolved quickly. Decided to try Tmobile for the unlimited data.. What a mistake. I've never been so dissatisfied with a company in my life and they refuse to correct the issue even tho the problem was the fault of a sales hungry associate who lied to make the sale. I bought 4 phones that are almost useless where we moved to 2 months after purchase. The company will do nothing to help even tho we were told there would be coverage in our new location and there isn't. Stay with a reputable company... Verizon is the only way to go.

I regret buying a T mobile prepaid plan. Visiting from Australia, I needed a quick SIM card with minimum data. I approached the guys at t mobile and one sales man told me I couldn't buy the minimum data which was 2gb I believe. He tried about 3 SIM cards that wouldn't work. The 4th finally went through, I was oversold onto a plan that I clearly didn't need, then tried to charge me $25 for a simple SIM card. To make matters worse the receipt wouldn't print so he promised to send the receipt to me via email. I knew he wouldn't, and he didn't let me down - he didn't. 
Before I committed to buy it I asked about coverage as I was going trekking throughout Utah. He said I'd have no problem except in canyons which I understand. Lo and behold I had No Service on the whole trip whereas my travel buddies on Verizon had coverage the whole time. Coverage was important to me because I'd be in locations by myself where I needed GPS. 
Then I used this same t mobile sim in New York where lag was rife and lost connection totally as soon as I walked into a building. 
Go Verizon - I wouldn't recommend t mobile at all.

I bought a new watch from a store in Portage Indiana it never worked so I tried tech support with no like the day 3 after trying to get the watch working I wanted to exchange it for same item but none In stock so I wanted to return it they wouldn't.  Called tech support 611 they said they couldn't do anything but would like to sell me another one at full price.   I would still have to fend for my self on the return.

I would rate it a "0"! My husband and I had the worst experience with your customer service department and with your in store representatives! We have been loyal customers for 7 years and we honestly never planned on going to another provider until today. My husband and I wanted to upgrade to the new IPhone 8 plus with 256 GB and we wanted to know the full details before we purchased them. We were outraged that all your representative would give us different prices regarding the deposit we had to leave down. My husband spoke to a total of 7 representatives in store and on your customer service department. Two representatives told him that I did not have to put any deposit down since I had jump on demand and then we got other representatives that would give us different prices ranging from  $177.99-$256? Why is no one on the same page? What kind of company gives so many different prices for the same phone? We asked for manager assistance and your reps would hesitate to transfer us!

I was told prior to canceling my prepaid account that I would receive a refund for remaining time (20 days) left that I did not use. So 30 days later I called today to find out when I could expect my refund and was told that I was misinformed and that they do not issue refunds for balance of prepaid accounts so they get to keep my $30. 

Worst customer service ever! Don't waste your time with T-Mobile unless you like being robbed!!

I called to get info about upgrading my current IPhone and got the terrible, unfair, weird news ever. If i use the Jump insurance I am paying and request a mew device even though I have to pay at least 50% of my current device all the payments I have been doing and will have to do to upgrade will be lost. T-Mobile won’t apply almost 200$ towards my new phone or even give me a credit and the worst part, I will have to start paying the new device from zero? this is stealing money from customers. also the sales taxes we pay to get this phone I am using right now won’t be used towards the sales taxes I will have to pay upfront when upgrading. I had to call them to get it back and over 4 weeks later I get a stupid pre-paid debit card to use. I want my money back!! I don't want some half-assed debit card!!   

I was a customer for about 10 years and this is how they treat you.

T-Mobile has the worst coverage!  It is 2017 and since I switched from Verizon to T-Mobile last year,  I cannot make or receive phone calls from my home because the coverage is so bad. I am in Phoenix and  I am 10 miles from the tower.  A rep from a T-Mobile store  told me their coverage isn't as good inside apartments as Verizon.   It is unbelievable that I cannot have cell phone coverage in a place I had it before moving to T-Mobile.  I am telling as many people as I can if they live in an apartment complex do not get T-Mobile or they will not be able to make or receive calls. I am a senior citizen and it's important I have access to a phone in case there's an emergency!!  Get out of the stone age T-Mobile!!!

I stayed 2 months in the USA for my summer vacation. I payed more that 55$ per month to have unlimited phone calls and texting, including 10Gb Internet. The places I travelled (Pittsburgh, Philadelphia, NYC, Clevaland, Miami, Seattle, Portland, Sacramento, San Francisco, Black Rock City) did not have Signal to call or message. It was horrible experience. It actually RUINED my vacation. My Internet data did not work in most of the time or was working super slow. I am sincerely UNHAPPY WITH T-MOBILE PHONE SERVICE IN THE USA. they should work a lot on its service.

Unfortunately it wouldn't let me give T-mobile 0 stars. They have the worst customer service I've ever encountered in my life. It is worse than I could have ever imagined for a company. They signed me up and charged me for a plan and phone I didn't want. I called within the hour and told them I didn't want the plan or phone. They said they would cancel the plan and not send the phone. They did not cancel the plan until I called 5 different times about it. They did send the phone I didn't want. I called and they gave me a UPS label I had to print out myself and take time out of my day to go to UPS and send. I received the phone in May, sent it back the next day, got an email that T-mobile received it.....in May. But they never gave me my money back! They charged me in full for the phone that I returned and never gave me my money back. I called 15-20 times, spoke to over 30 different representatives, and heard at least 5 personal guarantees from reps that they had solved my problem, but still it never was and they never sent me my refund. It's all pretty simple: I returned the phone, so please refund me my money. It is September now, more than 4 months after I returned the phone and still haven't gotten a refund. Not only does T-mobile owe me $130, but I can't get back the 10 hrs (at least) that I spent on the phone wasting my time with customer service reps (many of whom were nice people) who never solved my problem. Until T-mobile sends me a check for $530 ($130 for the phone and $400 for the 10 hrs of my time I wasted trying to fix the issue) I will not be satisfied. I've had a T-mobile phone for over 10 years, you'd think they'd have some customer loyalty.
